deck.academic_subtitle: Multimodal Vision-Language-Action Decision Support for False-Positive Safety Stops in Autonomous Guided Vehicles
affiliation_lines: Under guidance of Prof. Peter Nauth

slides.title: AGV Fleet VLA Intelligence
slides.subtitle: Multimodal Vision-Language-Action Decision Support for False-Positive Safety Stops in Autonomous Guided Vehicles
slides.bottom_note: Human-in-the-loop advisory system for operator review of AGV stop events. Prototype evidence, not autonomous actuation.

slides.title: Problem and Motivation
bullets: False-positive AGV stops reduce throughput and increase operator workload.
Range sensors can detect distance, but not whether the obstacle is harmless or dangerous.
Lightweight foil, wrapping film, paper, and soft barriers can trigger the same stop logic as real hazards.
Operators need faster context to distinguish nuisance stops from genuine risk without removing human authority.

left_card.title: Benign nuisance obstacles
items: Reflective foil or soft plastic sheet

right_card.title: High-risk obstacles
items: Solid equipment blocking the AGV path

slides.bottom_note: The presentation focuses on a narrow warehouse stop-resolution problem: improving operator review, not replacing the vehicle safety controller.

slides.title: Objective, Scope, and Contribution
slides.objective: Build a multimodal decision-support dashboard that helps an operator review AGV stop events using media, telemetry, and recent incident history.
contributions: Structured operator workflow and persistence layer
Conservative evaluation and CSV-backed audit framework

slides.title: System Architecture
bullets: Inputs: image or video frame, optional audio, typed telemetry, and recent incident summaries.
Processing: Gemini multimodal analysis with a constrained JSON response contract.
Outputs: danger score, recommended action, commentary, and UI trigger state.
Persistence: Firestore stores both model output and operator decision for later audit and reuse.
slides.caption: Functional diagram of the advisory pipeline from multimodal inputs to operator-facing recommendation and persistence.

slides.title: Prototype Workflow

steps.label: 1. Capture
steps.caption: Operator provides incident media and typed telemetry in the prototype UI.

steps.label: 2. Analyze
steps.caption: The advisory engine returns status, score, and recommended operator action.

steps.label: 3. Decide
steps.caption: The operator confirms the stop or forces an override; the final action is logged.

slides.bottom_note: The implemented workflow remains human-in-the-loop at the final decision point.

slides.title: Model Contract and Decision Taxonomy
config_items: Prompt includes multimodal evidence and recent incident context
score_mapping: Scores 3-5: Keep Stop / Do Not Override

slides.title: Persistence, Dashboard, and Traceability
bullets: Firestore stores incident context, model output, operator action, ratings, and notes.
Dashboard views aggregate total incidents, critical events, and low-score overrides.
Incident history supports CSV export and later audit of system recommendations versus operator actions.
History snapshots and source IDs are stored to make future consistency analysis traceable.

slides.title: Evaluation Design

right_stream.title: Operational audit
items: 3 repeated object groups and 9 repeated rows

slides.bottom_note: The current evidence is prototype-level and descriptive. It is not an industrial validation benchmark.

slides.title: Operational Audit Results
bullets: The repeated-case evidence is small but useful for descriptive consistency checks.
Only one repeated object cluster showed score variance in the export.
Two repeated object groups remained stable at score 4 across all three occurrences.
slides.source_note: Source: incident_history_audit.json generated from the exported dashboard CSV.

slides.title: Repeated-Case Findings
rows: PED-A
Force Override
Segregated lane; clean telemetry
PED-B
Force Override
Segregated lane; malformed lat/long
PED-C
Force Override
Segregated lane; clean telemetry
stable_groups: Blue hand truck group: 4, 4, 4 and operator kept stop.
Reflective silver cargo group: 4, 4, 4 across all three rows.
slides.bottom_note: Conclusion: the observed 2 versus 3 split is localized and input-sensitive, not evidence of broad nondeterminism.

slides.title: Challenges, Limitations, and Mitigations
rows: Free-tier quota and API project limits
Model-side reruns could not be scaled credibly
Reported the offline audit truthfully and kept a quota-safe probe harness ready
Small pilot benchmark
No strong statistical performance claim is justified
Framed results as prototype evidence and audit evidence only
Manual telemetry entry in the prototype
Operator-entered inputs can introduce noise or malformed fields
Included telemetry anomalies explicitly in the operational audit
Text-surrogate benchmark inputs
Benchmark does not fully match raw-media production conditions
Separated the pilot benchmark from the operational dashboard audit
No formal HCI field study
Operator trust and speed effects remain unvalidated
Positioned HCI evaluation as future work
slides.bottom_note: The advisory-only system boundary is intentional and remains appropriate given current VLM spatial-reasoning limits.

slides.title: Conclusion and Future Work
takeaways: A working multimodal AGV advisory prototype was implemented.
The strongest contribution is the operator workflow, persistence layer, and traceability design.
The present evidence is limited but honest, reproducible, and appropriate for a masters-level prototype.
future_work: Expand the benchmark from 4 scenarios to a wider and more varied case set.
Automate live telemetry ingestion through an API or streaming interface.
Move benchmark coverage from text surrogates to raw media inputs.
Run an HCI study on operator trust, explanation quality, and review speed.
slides.bottom_note: Best positioning: a credible academic prototype with a clear path to stronger evaluation.

slides.title: Appendix: Selected References
references: Ahn et al. (2022) - SayCan: grounded language for robotics.
Brohan et al. (2023) - RT-2: vision-language-action modeling for robots.
Tian et al. (2024) - DriveVLM: staged reasoning for autonomous driving.
Parr et al. (2024) - remote monitoring, assistance, management, and driving.
Guo et al. (2024) - SURDS: spatial reasoning limitations in multimodal models.
slides.note: The full bibliography and methodological detail are documented in the project report.
